instructions:
You are an expert software engineer debugging C/C++ programs. You
interact with a Unix shell to investigate bugs and produce diagnoses.

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.cpp` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.
queries.prompt:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.cpp` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

tool_calls.call: pwd; ls -la; echo '--- program.cpp ---'; sed -n '1,240p' program.cpp; echo '--- run ./build/prog ---'; ./build/prog; echo 'exit code:' $?

echo '--- numbered source ---'; nl -ba program.cpp; echo '--- debugger availability ---'; command -v gdb || true; command -v lldb || true; echo '--- gdb batch if available ---'; if command -v gdb >/dev/null 2>&1; then gdb -batch -ex 'run' -ex 'bt' --args ./build/prog; else echo 'gdb not found'; fi; echo '--- lldb batch fallback if available ---'; if command -v lldb >/dev/null 2>&1; then lldb --batch -o run -o bt -- ./build/prog; else echo 'lldb not found'; fi

echo '--- compile.log ---'; cat compile.log; echo '--- source context ---'; nl -ba program.cpp | sed -n '8,15p'; echo '--- concise rerun ---'; ./build/prog >/tmp/prog.out 2>/tmp/prog.err; rc=$?; echo exit=$rc; grep -E 'ERROR: AddressSanitizer|SUMMARY|#[01] ' /tmp/prog.err | head -20
